summary: The Iran conflict remains the dominant macro force, with the Strait of Hormuz still closed and Brent crude near $105 a barrel, even as Trump extended the U.S.-Iran ceasefire indefinitely and the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire by three weeks. Blowout chip earnings, led by Intel surging ~25% and AMD following, are reinforcing the AI demand narrative and driving the week's equity strength.
tweet: Brent at $105 as the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, but Intel's 22% AI revenue growth and Texas Instruments' results signal resilient chip demand, keeping stocks in greed territory even as consumers sour.
